question: Is OrganizationRole dynamic or only a template?
answer: TEMPLATE ONLY — used at create/invite/assign to copy fields onto membership; PermissionsGuard never reads OrganizationRole at request time.

question: Are permissions copied on assign?
answer: YES — assignRoleToUser writes role.permissions role.membershipRole roleLabel fieldAgentAccessDefault stationScopeDefault defaultStationIds onto membership.

question: What happens on later role template change?
answer: updateRole updates OrganizationRole row only — no membership cascade; no job; no version bump.

question: Are existing memberships updated?
answer: NO automatically. Only explicit assignRoleToUser or updateOrgUser permission/role patches.

question: Does a role version exist?
answer: NO — no roleVersion/permissionVersion column on OrganizationRole Membership JWT or refresh token.

question: Can membership diverge from role?
answer: YES — after copy; updateOrgUser can patch permissions/station/role independently; template edits leave snapshots stale.
evidence: users.service updateOrgUser; mapOrgUser still shows organizationRoleName

question: Are overrides explicit or implicit snapshots?
answer: IMPLICIT SNAPSHOTS — no override flag; membership.permissions IS the working copy. Cannot distinguish inherited vs customized.

question: Can UI show a role whose effective permissions no longer match?
answer: YES — organizationRoleId/roleLabel/name displayed while membership.permissions may differ from template; FE hasPermission uses login snapshot not template.

question: Can deactivated roles still be effective?
answer: YES for already-assigned members — deleteRole sets isActive=false but membership keeps copied permissions; listRoles hides inactive templates only.

question: How are system roles handled?
answer: Seeded per org via ensureDefaultRoles from DEFAULT_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_TEMPLATES (10 keys: org_admin sub_admin disposition accounting station_manager employee driver field_agent service read_only). isSystemTemplate=true systemKey set.

question: Can system roles be changed copied deleted?
answer: Rename blocked; permissions/membershipRole/station defaults blocked on system templates; description-only edits allowed. duplicateRole allowed (creates custom copy). deleteRole blocked for system templates. Soft-delete blocked if memberships still reference role (custom only).

question: Are roles org-unique?
answer: systemKey unique per org (@@unique organizationId+systemKey). Custom roles systemKey=null — multiple customs allowed; name uniqueness NOT enforced.

question: What is MembershipRole?
answer: Coarse enum ORG_ADMIN SUB_ADMIN WORKER DRIVER — carried on membership and into JWT; ORG_ADMIN bypasses module permissions.

question: What is roleLabel?
answer: Display string copied from OrganizationRole.name on assign; independently editable via updateOrgUser; not used for authz.

question: Station scope / stationIds / fieldAgentAccess
answer: Stored on membership (and role defaults). StationAccessService (feature-flagged stationsScopeV2Enabled): ORG_ADMIN bypass; else stationIds JSON; else stationScope ALL bypass; else SUB_ADMIN/WORKER treat scope string as single station id; DRIVER/others bypass. Separate from module permissions.

question: Canonical module keys
answer: PERMISSION_MODULE_KEYS in backend permission.constants.ts mirrored by FE PERMISSION_MODULES (same keys including payment submodules).

question: Does manage imply write and read?
answer: YES in evaluateModulePermission and FE hasPermission — centralized cascade.

question: Does write imply read?
answer: YES — same cascade in BE util and FE.

question: Is cascade central or per-guard?
answer: Central in permission.util.evaluateModulePermission; PermissionsGuard and assertMembershipPermission share it; FE duplicates logic.
preliminaryRisk: P1 — duplicated FE copy can drift

question: Unknown permission keys?
answer: Dropped by normalizeMembershipPermissions — never grant. evaluate returns false for unknown modules.

question: New modules default-denied?
answer: YES if not in membership JSON and not ORG_ADMIN/MASTER_ADMIN.

question: Can missing permission object allow?
answer: NO — null/missing flags → deny (except ORG_ADMIN/MASTER_ADMIN bypass).

question: Wildcards?
answer: NO wildcard support.

question: Is ORG_ADMIN full bypass?
answer: YES for PermissionsGuard and assertMembershipPermission within org. StationAccess also bypasses. FE hasPermission true for all modules.
preliminaryRisk: P1 — bypass ignores membership.permissions JSON entirely

question: Master admin routes separated?
answer: YES — /admin/* typically RolesGuard @Roles MASTER_ADMIN; OrgScopingGuard bypass for MASTER_ADMIN.

question: Can FE/BE use different permission names?
answer: Possible if constants diverge — currently mirrored by comment+manual lists; no shared package. Unknown FE keys fail closed on BE after normalize.

question: Customer as IAM role?
answer: Customer is a business entity not MembershipRole — no customer portal role in this IAM model.

question: RolesGuard without @Roles?
answer: Returns true (auth-only). Many org controllers use OrgScopingGuard+RolesGuard without @Roles or PermissionsGuard → any ACTIVE member can call writes.
